title: Pleiarina mucronata
intro: Pleiarina mucronata, also known as Bitterleaf, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rutaceae, commonly found in tropical regions of Asia. The health benefits of Pleiarina mucronata include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include limonoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pleiarina mucronata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Pleiarina mucronata belongs to the family of flowering plants. It is classified under the order Lamiales. Within this order, it is further classified under the subfamily Scrophularioideae. This classification places it within the family Orobanchaceae. Further research may be needed to confirm its exact classification within the family.
